title: Two divergent copies of the app and test suite ship in the workspace
evidence: app.py vs app_modified.py (differ only at line 163: port 5000 vs 5001); test_app.py vs test_app_fixed.py (test_app_fixed.py adds temp-DB monkeypatching). Agent stdout names app_modified.py 'the main file' while README.md and tests target app.py
suggestion: Ship a single app module and a single test module; the agent wrote *_modified/*_fixed because it could not overwrite the scaffolded app.py/test_app.py (see _agent_stdout.log write refusals)

title: Flask app runs with debug=True bound to 0.0.0.0
evidence: app.py:163 app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000); same in app_modified.py:163
suggestion: Disable the Werkzeug debugger (debug=False) for any non-local deployment — debug=True exposes an interactive console enabling remote code execution

title: Build artifacts committed into the run workspace
evidence: books.db (12KB SQLite file) and .coverage present alongside source
suggestion: Exclude generated DB and coverage files from the deliverable

title: ?author= filter uses substring LIKE match rather than exact match
evidence: app.py:76 SELECT * FROM books WHERE author LIKE ? with pattern f'%{author}%'
suggestion: Acceptable per R3 (a filter exists); note that 'Orwell' matches 'George Orwell' by substring, which the tests rely on
